nodes.label: The Last Halloween
nodes.summary: The Last Halloween is an American Halloween television special produced by Hanna-Barbera. It premiered and first aired on CBS on October 28, 1991. The visual effects and animation were provided by Industrial Light & Magic and Pacific Data Images.

nodes.label: Paul R. Hill
nodes.summary: Paul Richard Hill (1909–1990) was a mid–twentieth-century American aerodynamicist. He was a leading research and development engineer and manager for NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration) and its predecessor, NACA (the National Advisory Council for Aeronautics) between 1939 and 1970,

nodes.label: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects
nodes.summary: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects is a 1956 book by then-retired Air Force UFO investigator Edward J. Ruppelt, detailing his experience running Project Blue Book. nodes.label: Buzz Aldrin
nodes.summary: American astronaut; second man on moon (Apollo 11, 1969). 33rd-degree Scottish Rite Mason. Performed first religious ceremony on moon — Scottish Rite communion with silk flag.

sources.quote: Apollo 11 astronaut; 33rd-degree Mason; performed first lunar communion

nodes.label: Neil Armstrong
nodes.summary: American astronaut and naval aviator; first person to walk on the moon (Apollo 11, 1969).

sources.quote: Apollo 11 astronaut; first man on moon

nodes.label: Scottish Rite Freemasonry
nodes.summary: Esoteric branch of Freemasonry with 33 degrees symbolically tied to the 33 vertebrae of the spine culminating in the crown chakra (divine insight gateway). Buzz Aldrin held the 33rd degree.

sources.timestamp_end: Esoteric branch of Freemasonry with 33 degrees tied to spinal chakras

nodes.label: The Overview Effect
nodes.summary: Spiritual/cognitive shift astronauts report from seeing Earth from space; first identified via Edgar Mitchell — Earth's dynamic too beautiful to be accidental, implying a creator 'above the religions.'

sources.timestamp_end: Astronauts' spiritual transformation from seeing Earth from space

nodes.label: Institute of Noetic Sciences (IONS)
nodes.summary: Think tank dedicated to consciousness, psychic phenomena, and metaphysics founded by Apollo 14 astronaut Edgar Mitchell. Wernher von Braun attended early fundraising dinners and encouraged the effort.

sources.timestamp_end: Edgar Mitchell's think tank for consciousness research

nodes.label: Buzz Aldrin carried a small silk flag hand-stitched with the Scottish Rite symbol of Freemasonry to
nodes.summary: Buzz Aldrin carried a small silk flag hand-stitched with the Scottish Rite symbol of Freemasonry to the moon; Masonic Grand Lodge of Ohio claims Apollo 11 established an appendant body of Freemasonry on the moon.

sources.quote: Among his belongings was a small silk flag hand-stitched with the symbol of the Scottish Rite of Freemasonry. According to the Masonic Grand Lodge of Ohio, the Apollo 11 mission even established an appendant body of the Masonic Lodge on the moon.

type_specific.statement_text: Buzz Aldrin carried a small silk flag hand-stitched with the Scottish Rite symbol of Freemasonry to the moon; Masonic Grand Lodge of Ohio claims Apollo 11 established an appendant body of Freemasonry on the moon.

nodes.label: Buzz Aldrin performed the first religious ceremony on the moon — communion with wine, bread, and sil
nodes.summary: Buzz Aldrin performed the first religious ceremony on the moon — communion with wine, bread, and silver chalice before his first moonwalk. The wine curled 'slowly and gracefully' up the side of the cup.

sources.quote: Aldrin then proceeded to carry out the first religious ceremony ever conducted on the moon. Using a small silver chalice, a bit of wine, and a piece of bread. He recalled the wine curling slowly and gracefully up the side of the cup.

type_specific.statement_text: Buzz Aldrin performed the first religious ceremony on the moon — communion with wine, bread, and silver chalice before his first moonwalk. The wine curled 'slowly and gracefully' up the side of the cup.

nodes.label: On Apollo 14, Edgar Mitchell ran a private ESP experiment attempting to send mental images back to E
nodes.summary: On Apollo 14, Edgar Mitchell ran a private ESP experiment attempting to send mental images back to Earth using cards; results were scored better than chance.

sources.quote: On his journey to the moon, Mitchell conducted a private ESP experiment attempting to send mental images back to Earth using a set of cards. The results scored by colleagues back on Earth were better than chance.

type_specific.statement_text: On Apollo 14, Edgar Mitchell ran a private ESP experiment attempting to send mental images back to Earth using cards; results were scored better than chance.

nodes.label: Per Jesse: von Braun deeply supported Mitchell's consciousness/psi interests and encouraged him to f
nodes.summary: Per Jesse: von Braun deeply supported Mitchell's consciousness/psi interests and encouraged him to find a site within NASA to study consciousness further; von Braun attended early fundraising dinners for Mitchell's Institute of Noetic Sciences.

sources.quote: Warner von Braun — according to Mitchell, deeply supportive, even encouraging Mitchell to find a site within NASA to study consciousness further. Von Braun attended one of its early fundraising dinners.

type_specific.statement_text: Per Jesse: von Braun deeply supported Mitchell's consciousness/psi interests and encouraged him to find a site within NASA to study consciousness further; von Braun attended early fundraising dinners for Mitchell's Institute of Noetic Sciences.

nodes.label: Mitchell's Overview Effect framing: Earth from space is 'too much purpose, too much logic, too beaut
nodes.summary: Mitchell's Overview Effect framing: Earth from space is 'too much purpose, too much logic, too beautiful to have happened by accident. There has to be a creator of the universe who stands above the religions.'

sources.quote: The Earth dynamic, overwhelming — it was just too beautiful to have happened by accident. There has to be a creator of the universe who stands above the religions that we ourselves create to govern our lives.

type_specific.statement_text: Mitchell's Overview Effect framing: Earth from space is 'too much purpose, too much logic, too beautiful to have happened by accident. There has to be a creator of the universe who stands above the religions.'
